The \(K'(X)_i\) thus defined are additive subgroups of \(K'(X)\).

Indeed, let \(x,x'\in K'(X)_i\), and, for a given closed subset \(Y\) in \(X\), let \(M^\bullet\) and \(M'^\bullet\) be the corresponding complexes; then \(x+x'\) is the class of the complex \(M^\bullet\oplus M'^\bullet\), and

\[ \begin{aligned} \operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet\oplus M'^\bullet)) &=\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet)\oplus H^*(M'^\bullet))\\ &=\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet))\cup\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M'^\bullet)) ; \end{aligned} \]
since the codimension of a finite union of closed subsets is the infimum of the codimensions of the closed subsets, we indeed have \(x+x'\in K'(X)_i\).

blocks.title: Proposition
blocks.html:
Proposition 6.6.

The filtration 5.5 of \(K'(X)\) is a ring filtration and \(K'(X)_{d+1}=0\).

Let \(x\in K'(X)_i\), \(x'\in K'(X)_j\), and let \(Y\) be a closed subset of \(X\). There exists a complex \(M^\bullet\) such that \(x=\operatorname{cl}(M^\bullet)\), and \(\operatorname{codim}(\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet))\cap Y,Y)\geq i\); there exists a complex \(M'^\bullet\) such that \(x'=\operatorname{cl}(M'^\bullet)\), and:
\[ \operatorname{codim}(\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M'^\bullet))\cap\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet))\cap Y, \operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet))\cap Y)\geq j. \]
We then have: \(xx'=\operatorname{cl}(M^\bullet\mathbin{\mathop\otimes^{\mathbf L}}M'^\bullet)\), and:
\[ \operatorname{codim}(\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet))\cap\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M'^\bullet))\cap Y,Y)\geq i+j \qquad(\mathrm{EGA}\ 0_{\mathrm{IV}}\ 14.2.2.3). \]
It therefore suffices, to complete the proof, to note that:
\[ \operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet\mathbin{\mathop\otimes^{\mathbf L}}M'^\bullet)) \subset\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet))\cap\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M'^\bullet)). \]

To show that \(K'(X)_{d+1}\) is zero, we take \(x\in K'(X)_{d+1}\), and \(Y=X\); there then exists \(M^\bullet\) such that \(x=\operatorname{cl}(M^\bullet)\), and \(\operatorname{codim}_X(\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet)))\geq d+1\); since \(X\) has dimension \(d\), this implies \(\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(M^\bullet))=\varnothing\), hence \(M^\bullet\) is acyclic, and \(x=0\).

blocks.title: Lemma
blocks.html:
Lemma 6.7.

Let \(X=\operatorname{Spec}(A)\) be a noetherian affine scheme, \(x_1,\ldots,x_k\) a finite number of points of \(X\), \(E\) an invertible \(\mathcal O_X\)-Module. Then there exists a section \(s\in\Gamma(X,E)\) such that for every \(i\), \(s_{x_i}\) is a basis of \(E_{x_i}\).

Let us set \(M=\Gamma(X,E)\). For every \(i\), let \(x'_i\) be a closed point of \(X\) such that \(x'_i\in\overline{\{x_i\}}\). The \(x'_i\) correspond to maximal ideals \(\mathfrak m_i\) of \(A\), which we may assume all distinct, changing if necessary the index set so that each of the \(\mathfrak m_i\) occurs once and only once in the family \((\mathfrak m_i)\). Since \(E\) is invertible, we have for every \(i\) an isomorphism \(M_{\mathfrak m_i}\simeq A_{\mathfrak m_i}\), from which we deduce an isomorphism \(M/\mathfrak m_iM\simeq A/\mathfrak m_i\), then \(\prod_i M/\mathfrak m_iM\simeq\prod_i A/\mathfrak m_i\), or again \(A/\prod_i\mathfrak m_i\simeq M/(\prod_i\mathfrak m_i)M\). Lifting this last isomorphism, we obtain a homomorphism \(A\longrightarrow M\) such that the corresponding homomorphism \(A_{\mathfrak m_i}\longrightarrow M_{\mathfrak m_i}\) is, for every \(i\), an isomorphism modulo \(\mathfrak m_i\), hence an isomorphism by Nakayama. The image of 1 under this homomorphism therefore gives a section \(s\) of \(E\) which is a basis of \(E_{x'_i}\) for every \(i\). Since the set of points where \(s\) is a basis of \(E\) is an open subset of \(X\), \(s\) is also a basis of \(E_{x_i}\) for \(i=1,\ldots,k\).

blocks.title: Lemma
blocks.html:
Lemma 6.8.

Let \(X\) be a noetherian scheme having an ample sheaf \(L\), \(E\) an invertible \(\mathcal O_X\)-Module, \(x_1,\ldots,x_k\) points of \(X\). Then there exists an
integer \(n\) such that for every \(k\geq1\), there exists a section \(s\in\Gamma(X,E(kn))\) which is a basis of \(E(kn)_{x_i}\) for every \(i\).

We can find an integer \(n_0\), and a section \(f\in\Gamma(X,L^{\otimes n_0})\) such that for every \(i\), \(x_i\in X_f\), and such that \(X_f\) is an affine open subset (EGA II 4.5.4). On \(X_f\), we can find a section \(t\in\Gamma(X_f,E)\) satisfying the conditions of Lemma 6.7; moreover, there exists an integer \(n'_0\) such that the section \(f^{\otimes n'_0}\otimes t\in\Gamma(X_f,E(n_0n'_0))\) extends to all of \(X\). Let us set \(n=n_0n'_0\); then \(f^{\otimes n'_0}\in\Gamma(X,L^{\otimes n})\); by taking the tensor product of the section of \(E(n)\) obtained above with \(f^{\otimes kn'_0}\), we thus obtain a section of \(E((k+1)n)\) for every \(k\geq0\). Since for every \(p\), \(X_{f^{\otimes p}}=X_f\), and the section \(t\) is a basis of \(E_{x_i}\) for every \(i\), the sections of \(E(kn)\) thus constructed for every \(k\geq1\) form a basis of \(E(kn)_{x_i}\) for every \(i\).

blocks.title: Theorem
blocks.html:
Theorem 6.9.

Let \(X\) be a noetherian scheme of dimension \(d\), having an ample sheaf \(L\). Then \(\operatorname{Fil}^{d+1}K'(X)=0\), and a fortiori \((\operatorname{Fil}^1K'(X))^{d+1}=0\).
(This last relation is due to J. P. Serre, in the general case considered here).
We will first show that if \(E,E'\) are two invertible sheaves on \(X\), with classes \(E,E'\), then \(E-E'\in K'(X)_1\).

Let \(Y\) be a closed subset of \(X\), \(Y_1,\ldots,Y_k\) its irreducible components, and for every \(i\), let \(x_i\) be a point of \(Y_i-\bigcup_{j\neq i}Y_j\). By Lemma 6.8, there exist integers \(n,n'\) such that, whatever the integers \(k,k'\geq1\), we can find homomorphisms from \(\mathcal O_X\) to \(E(kn)\), \(E'(k'n')\) which are isomorphisms at the points \(x_i\); we therefore obtain morphisms:
\[ L^{\otimes-kn}\xrightarrow{\ s_k\ }E,\qquad L^{\otimes-k'n'}\xrightarrow{\ s'_{k'}\ }E', \]
which are isomorphisms at the points \(x_i\). Let us then consider the complex:
\[ N^\bullet=\quad\cdots\longrightarrow0\longrightarrow L^{\otimes-nn'}\xrightarrow{(s'_{n},0)} E'\oplus L^{\otimes-nn'}\xrightarrow{0+s_{n'}}E\longrightarrow0\longrightarrow\cdots \]

The class of \(N^\bullet\) is \(E-E'\); moreover, \(N^\bullet\) is acyclic at the points \(x_i\), hence \(\operatorname{Supp}(H^*(N^\bullet))\cap Y\) contains no irreducible component of \(Y\), and consequently has codimension \(\geq1\) in \(Y\); this shows that \(E-E'\) is in \(K'(X)_1\).
Let now \(x\) be an element of \(\operatorname{Fil}^{d+1}K'(X)\); we will show that \(x=0\). We may assume that \(x\) can be written:
\[ x=\gamma^{i_1}(E_1-F_1)\cdots\gamma^{i_k}(E_k-F_k), \qquad\text{avec }i_1+\cdots+i_k\geq d+1, \]

the \(E_i,F_i\) being the classes of locally free sheaves of finite type, and, for every \(i\), \(E_i\) and \(F_i\) having the same rank. Moreover, decomposing \(X\) if necessary into a sum of a finite number of open sets, and arguing in each open set, we may assume the \(E_i,F_i\) of constant rank. Let then \(X'\) be the fibered product over \(X\) of the flag schemes of the \(E_i,F_i\). In \(K'(X')\), the images of the \(E_i,F_i\) are sums of classes of invertible sheaves. Let \(\xi,\eta\) be the classes of two invertible \(\mathcal O_{X'}\)-Modules; we have:
\[ \gamma_t(\xi-\eta)=\gamma_t(\xi-1)/\gamma_t(\eta-1) =\frac{1+(\xi-1)t}{1+(\eta-1)t}. \]

Hence \(\gamma^i(\xi-\eta)=(-1)^{i-1}(\xi-\eta)(\eta-1)^{i-1}\). Now \(X'\) is noetherian and has an ample sheaf, so, by what precedes and 6.6, \(\gamma^i(\xi-\eta)\in K'(X')_i\). Using the additivity of \(\gamma_t\) and 6.6, we deduce that \(f^*(\gamma^j(E_j-F_j))\in K'(X')_j\), and \(f^*(x)\in K'(X')_{d+1}\), \(f\) being the canonical morphism \(X'\longrightarrow X\).

We will deduce from this that \(f^*(x)=0\). Let \(r\) be the relative dimension of \(X'\) over \(X\). There exists an element \(x'\) of \(K'(X')\), belonging to a basis of \(K'(X')\) over \(K'(X)\), such that:
\[ x'=\prod_{i=0}^{r}(\xi_i-1), \]

the \(\xi_i\) being the classes of invertible sheaves; to show this, we reduce, by the argument of 3.1, to the case of a projective bundle over \(X\). Now this last case is an immediate consequence of 1.13. The element \(x'\) considered is in \(K'(X')_r\) by what precedes, and consequently \(x'f^*(x)\in K'(X')_{d+r+1}\). Now \(\dim X'=d+r\); hence by 6.6, \(x'f^*(x)=0\). Since \(x'\) is part of a basis of \(K'(X')\) over \(K'(X)\), this implies \(f^*(x)=0\). Since \(f^*\) is injective (3.1), we finally have \(x=0\).

blocks.title: Problem
blocks.html:
Problem 6.10.

Under the hypotheses of 6.9, is it true that for every \(k\) we have: \(\operatorname{Fil}^k K'(X)\subset K'(X)_k\)?
BIBLIOGRAPHY

[1] Bass H., K-Theory and stable algebra, Publ. Math. de l'IHES, n°22.

[2] Chevalley C., Séminaire 1958 : Anneaux de Chow et applications.

[3] Hakim M., Topos localement annelés et schémas relatifs, thesis (in preparation).

[4] Hartshorne R., Residues and duality, Lectures notes in Mathematics n°20, Springer-Verlag.

[5] Serre J.-P., Le groupe de Grothendieck des schémas en groupes réductifs, Publications Mathématiques de l'IHES, n°34.
